requirements.QT-CTRL-001: A View control switches between the three views — central tendency, outlier scatter, and categorical exceedance — rendering one styled, clickable row per view with the active view marked.
requirements.QT-CTRL-002: A Correction control offers the configured parameter/correction options (default QTcF, QTcB, Heart Rate) and selects start_measure (QTcF) on first render, falling back to the first available measure when start_measure is unlisted.
requirements.QT-CTRL-003: Statistic (mean / median), display (Δ / ΔΔ), timepoint, and categorical filter controls narrow the view; applyFilters constrains rows on the active selections only, so an empty selection constrains nothing.
requirements.QT-CT-001: The central-tendency view plots per-arm change-from-baseline over ordered study visits (visits sorted by numeric visit), one series per treatment arm.
requirements.QT-CT-002: In mean mode the Δ series carries each arm's mean change with a two-sided 90% CI at every visit; in median mode the series carries no CI (lo/hi NaN). The chart draws per-arm lines with the CI band.
requirements.QT-CT-003: A mode-labelled horizontal reference line marks the ICH-E14 threshold of regulatory concern on the central-tendency chart.
requirements.QT-CT-004: The placebo-corrected ΔΔ mode drops the placebo arm and subtracts the placebo mean change at each visit; the placebo arm is auto-detected (matching /placebo/i) and ordered first.
requirements.QT-CT-005: The ICH-E14 metric is the largest upper bound of the two-sided 90% CI for the placebo-corrected mean difference (ΔΔ) across visits, flagged against the ~10 ms reference; the metric is reported only in mean + ΔΔ mode.
requirements.QT-CT-006: Each arm's peak-effect visit — the visit of maximum effect — is marked on the central-tendency chart.
requirements.QT-CT-007: Heart rate is offered as a central-tendency measure without the QTc ICH-E14 reference line (the ~10 ms threshold applies to QTc only).
requirements.QT-OUT-001: The outlier scatter plots one point per subject at the subject's maximum post-baseline QTc value against their baseline QTc.
requirements.QT-OUT-002: With a specific timepoint selected the scatter picks that visit's row per subject (matching a numeric-typed visit against a string timepoint); otherwise the maximum post-baseline value is used.
requirements.QT-OUT-003: The outlier scatter draws the 450/480/500 ms absolute-QTc diagonals, and in per-visit mode adds the 30/60 ms change-from-baseline horizontal lines; the absolute cut-lines apply only to QTc corrections.
requirements.QT-OUT-004: Each subject point is coloured and shaped by treatment arm, with a distinct cycling point style per arm.
requirements.QT-OUT-005: The scatter carries a zero no-change reference line, correction-suffixed axis titles (change-from-baseline vs baseline QTc, in the measure's units), and a padded domain that keeps the diagonals and change lines in view.
requirements.QT-OUT-006: The scatter point tooltip lists the subject id, arm, baseline value, on-treatment value, change from baseline, and visit.
requirements.QT-OUT-007: Heart rate shows a QTc-only note in the outlier scatter view, since the absolute and change diagonals apply to QTc corrections only.
requirements.QT-CAT-001: The categorical view hides the chart and tabulates by-arm exceedance, with denominators of post-baseline subjects per arm, tracking each subject's maximum value and maximum change independently.
requirements.QT-CAT-002: Absolute-QTc thresholds (>450 / >480 / >500 ms) are counted per arm with a strict greater-than, so a value exactly at a threshold (e.g. 500) is excluded.
requirements.QT-CAT-003: Change-from-baseline thresholds (>30 / >60 ms) are counted per arm using each subject's maximum change, independently of the maximum value.
requirements.QT-DATA-001: The default settings carry the ADEG column mapping — a participant id column giving one point per subject and the exceedance denominators, plus arm, visit, measure, value, baseline, and change columns.
requirements.QT-DATA-002: The measure column holds the ECG parameter name and is matched against the correction/parameter options, so only the selected correction's records feed the active view.
requirements.QT-DATA-003: cleanData drops rows with a missing or non-numeric value and reports the removed count; the on-page removed-count path leaves no page errors.
requirements.QT-DATA-004: Change from baseline is the source change column when present, and is otherwise derived as value − baseline when that column is blank.
requirements.QT-DATA-005: checkInputs validates the schema's required column settings (measure_col, value_col, arm_col, baseline_col) against the remapped names and throws naming every missing column; the baseline record is identified by the baseline flag (ABLFL=Y) and excluded from the post-baseline extremes, and when no baseline-flag column is present a change-of-0 row is treated as the baseline.
requirements.QT-DATA-006: measuresPresent lists the distinct measures actually present in the data, so the correction control offers only measures the dataset carries.
requirements.QT-CFG-001: syncSettings fills defaults and normalizes field-list settings (arrayifying single values).
requirements.QT-CFG-002: syncSettings sorts and numifies the threshold lists (absolute_thresholds, change_thresholds).
requirements.QT-CFG-003: syncSettings falls back start_measure to the first available measure when the configured start_measure is not in the measure list.
requirements.QT-CFG-004: syncSettings rejects an out-of-range ci_level, keeping the default (0.9).
requirements.QT-CFG-005: The VIEWS list and the TIMEPOINT_MAX ("maximum post-baseline") sentinel are stable constants the controls and timepoint selection read from.
requirements.QT-CFG-006: zForCi returns the two-sided z multiplier for a confidence level, interpolating between table points and clamping at the ends.
requirements.QT-CFG-007: resolvePlaceboArm prefers the explicit placebo setting when present and otherwise auto-detects the placebo arm by matching /placebo/i.
requirements.QT-STAT-001: The unique helper keeps first-seen non-empty values, preserving order for the arm/visit/measure lists.
requirements.QT-STAT-002: The statistics helpers (mean, sd, quantile, median) match hand-computed values, backing the central-tendency means, the CI, and the categorical summaries.
requirements.QT-SCL-001: correctionSuffix maps the QTc formulas to their axis-title suffix (QTcF → Fridericia, QTcB → Bazett).
requirements.QT-SCL-002: isQtcMeasure and measureUnit distinguish a QTc correction (msec, cut-lines apply) from heart rate (bpm, no QTc cut-lines).
requirements.QT-SCL-003: The central-tendency axis title carries the mode prefix (Δ / ΔΔ) plus the correction suffix and units.
requirements.QT-SCL-004: The outlier-scatter axis titles mirror the design mockup (baseline QTc on x, change-from-baseline on y, with the correction suffix).
requirements.QT-SCL-005: formatNumber trims trailing zeros and guards non-finite values in tick and tooltip formatting.
requirements.QT-SCL-006: formatSigned signs the change value with a real minus glyph (−) for the tooltip and change labels.
requirements.QT-SCL-007: paddedDomain covers the data values plus any forced inclusions (the threshold cut-lines), and handles an empty or degenerate sample without collapsing.
requirements.QT-SCL-008: The scatter assigns a distinct cycling point style per arm so arms are distinguishable by shape as well as colour.
requirements.QT-PLG-001: hexToRgba converts a hex colour to an rgba string at a given opacity, backing the CI band and point fills.
requirements.QT-PLG-002: armColorScale assigns stable, cycling colours per arm, consistent across the central-tendency, scatter, and categorical views.
requirements.QT-PLG-003: scatterTooltip renders the outlier-point tooltip lines — id, arm, baseline, value, change, and visit.
requirements.QT-API-001: The public safety.viz collection exposes a `qtExplorer` factory function, identical to the named `qtExplorer` export.
